region_of_origin: India (Punjab / North India); served in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for stuffed paratha; 'flatbread food product' (FOODON_03302397) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for stuffed paratha; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 'Bread (substance)' is used as the closest parent; no specific stuffed paratha concept exists in SNOMED CT.

main_ingredients: potato (aloo) or paneer (Indian cottage cheese) or cauliflower (gobi)

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium stuffed paratha weighing approximately 120 g, typically served with yoghurt (dahi), pickle (achaar), or butter.

nutrient_source.title: Paratha, stuffed with potato (Aloo Paratha) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN reports nutrient values for aloo paratha (stuffed potato flatbread) made with whole wheat flour and ghee. Values are used as the primary reference for the aloo variant and as a representative composite for the paneer and gobi variants, which have similar macronutrient profiles. Paneer variant is slightly higher in protein and fat; gobi variant is slightly lower in energy. A composite mid-point estimate is used here. Values are per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Chapati / whole wheat flatbread GI — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for stuffed paratha in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 52 is estimated from whole wheat chapati / roti values (GI range 48–62, mean ~52) reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care 44(Suppl 1):S1–S13). Stuffed paratha shares the same whole wheat flour base; the filling (potato, paneer, or cauliflower) and ghee topping modestly lower the overall GI relative to plain white bread. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 46.2) / 100 ≈ 24.0, rounded to 23.8 using 120 g serving × 38.5 g carb per 100 g = 46.2 g carb.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) and ghee-derived saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one paratha and avoid additional salt or high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles (achaar), as the combined sodium load can approach 400–500 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~24 per serving) from whole wheat carbohydrate base
type2_diabetes.guidance: The whole wheat flour base provides a lower GI (~52) than refined flour alternatives, but the substantial carbohydrate load per serving (~46 g) means blood glucose response should be monitored; pairing with a protein-rich side such as dahi (yoghurt) or dal can further blunt postprandial glycaemia.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee (~4.8 g/100 g) and overall energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The ghee topping contributes saturated fat that may raise LDL cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should request minimal ghee or substitute with a small amount of unsaturated oil, and limit consumption to one paratha per meal occasion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stuffed Paratha (Aloo / Paneer / Gobi) is a traditional North Indian whole wheat flatbread that is filled with a spiced stuffing of either mashed potato (aloo), crumbled Indian cottage cheese (paneer), or seasoned cauliflower (gobi), then pan-fried on a cast-iron griddle (tawa) and finished with a generous topping of hot ghee (clarified butter). The dough is made from whole wheat flour (atta), water, and salt, rolled thin, filled, sealed, and re-rolled before cooking. The result is a multi-layered, slightly crisp exterior with a soft, flavourful interior. Taste is mildly spiced and buttery; texture is flaky on the outside and tender within. Stuffed paratha is a staple breakfast and lunch dish across Punjab and North India, and is widely served in Indian restaurants in Singapore. Nutritionally, it provides moderate energy (~265 kcal/100 g), complex carbohydrates from whole wheat (~38.5 g/100 g), modest protein (~7.2 g/100 g), and fat primarily from ghee (~9.5 g/100 g, of which ~4.8 g is saturated). The whole wheat base contributes dietary fibre (~3.2 g/100 g) and B vitamins. Clinically, the moderate glycemic index (~52) makes it preferable to refined-flour breads for blood glucose management, though portion control remains important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The ghee content warrants attention for those managing cardiovascular disease or hypertension. Commonly served with plain yoghurt, mango pickle, or butter.
common_user_queries: How many calories in aloo paratha?
What is the difference between aloo paratha and gobi paratha?
Is paratha made with whole wheat?
How much fat is in paneer paratha?
What is the GI of paratha?
Can I eat paratha if I have high blood pressure?
What is the serving size of paratha?

confidence_reason: ICMR-NIN provides nutrient data for aloo paratha (stuffed potato flatbread with whole wheat flour and ghee), which is the closest approved primary source. The record covers a composite of three filling variants (aloo, paneer, gobi); the aloo variant is the best-matched. Paneer and gobi variants have slightly different macronutrient profiles but are within a defensible range of the composite estimate. GI is estimated from whole wheat chapati analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021, as no direct paratha GI measurement is available. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. This record covers a composite of three filling variants: aloo (potato), paneer (Indian cottage cheese), and gobi (cauliflower). The aloo variant is the primary ICMR-NIN anchor; paneer and gobi variants are within ±10% of the composite macro values. 2. Vitamin B12 is 0.0 g as stuffed paratha is a plant-based and dairy-based dish with no meat or fish; the ghee and paneer (paneer variant) contribute negligible B12 not detectable at the 100 g level in ICMR-NIN tables — confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN for the aloo variant. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food. 5. GI is an analogous estimate from whole wheat chapati/roti values (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021); no direct paratha GI measurement was found in approved sources. 6. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Grains and staples', as stuffed paratha is a wheat-based flatbread dish. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from ICMR-NIN aloo paratha data and USDA SR Legacy whole wheat flatbread entries as supporting references.
